summary: The team discussed building a restaurant app where customers vote on menu items to refine offerings over time. Key areas included voting simplicity, authentication approach, and a dual-interface system for customers and staff. Data tracking and analytics were emphasized to support decision-making and inventory optimization. UX simplicity and mobile performance were highlighted as critical. Sarah will consolidate requirements for the next session.

details: Customers vote or rate menu items
Menu evolves based on popularity
Objective is to optimize offerings and reduce waste
Must be simple and quick (1–2 taps)
Options discussed: thumbs up/down, star ratings, reactions
Preference for visual and intuitive interaction
Debate between anonymous vs authenticated voting
Anonymous voting risks unreliable data
Lightweight login (Google/Apple) suggested as balance
Customer app sections: Home/About, Menu, Contact, Location
UX must be clean, fast, and mobile-friendly
Encourage voting through subtle prompts
Dual interface: customer-facing app and staff dashboard
Customers see menu and ratings only
Staff access ingredients, inventory, and analytics
Ingredient visibility restricted to staff
Track voting trends over time
Identify top-rated and low-performing dishes
Potential alerts for underperforming items
Strong data tracking required from the beginning
Link dish popularity to ingredient usage
Support stock level recommendations
Requires backend tracking of ingredients per dish
Backend must support scalability
Future phases may include notifications

decisions: Adopt a simple and quick voting system
Implement a dual-interface system (customer and staff)
Restrict ingredient visibility to staff only
Ensure strong data tracking from the start

action_items.task: Consolidate discussion into initial product requirements

open_questions.question: Which specific voting method will be implemented (stars, reactions, thumbs up/down)?
Should voting be anonymous or require authentication in the final version?
How will ingredient tracking be structured in the backend system?
What is the best strategy to encourage user participation in voting?
